title: Implement module lifecycle cleanup milestone

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.hard_constraints: Implement the GitHub milestone on one PR without adding public third-party loading or preserving preset compatibility aliases.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact runtime shape, tests, docs compression, generated-reference refresh, and issue close wording as long as the milestone acceptance criteria are met.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct implementation requires dynamic third-party module loading, package distribution redesign, or unrelated module internals.
canonical_core.next_action: Replace preset-driven module selection with config-owned modules.enabled and update registry/docs/tests.
proof_expectations: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_lifecycle_cli.py tests/test_workspace_report_cli.py tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py tests/test_workspace_packaging.py tests/test_workspace_skills_cli.py tests/test_workspace_ownership_cli.py
completion_criteria: Fresh init writes [modules].enabled = ["planning", "memory"] and installs those core modules.
Explicit --modules, including none, writes the repo-owned enabled module list.
Install, uninstall, upgrade, report, doctor, status, and start default from modules.enabled rather than presets or installed signals.
Registry/schema/docs remove preset/profile/feature-tier runtime selection concepts and define core-module terminology plus future external-module boundary.
Tests cover config-owned enablement, residue/missing classification, dependency/conflict reporting, and rejected preset surfaces.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Implement GitHub milestone Module lifecycle cleanup (#1514-#1520) on one PR.
intent.constraints: No public third-party module loading, no preset compatibility layer, and no unrelated module-internal redesign.
intent.latitude: Choose exact field names, output shape, docs compression, and test placement while satisfying milestone criteria.
intent.escalation: Escalate when implementation requires dynamic external module support, package distribution redesign, or unrelated subsystem rewrites.

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: One PR implementing modules.enabled as the lifecycle authority and removing preset/bootstrapper framing from runtime, registry, docs, and tests.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace runtime/config/schema/contracts/generated workspace package, README/docs/reference, focused tests, and this planning artifact.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Broad but bounded milestone PR; keep changes to directly referenced runtime, contract, generated, docs, and test surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest suite, generated command package check, and agentic-workspace proof for changed paths.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing package distribution, adding public external module loading, or rewriting module internals outside lifecycle selection.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated planning/memory package internals, model harness suites, and non-module product areas.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.

context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from config-owned modules.enabled as the root lifecycle authority; remove preset runtime selection and prove all milestone issue criteria.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.escalate when: A correct path requires package distribution redesign, external SDK design, or unrelated subsystem rewrites.

completion_criteria: All open issues in the Module lifecycle cleanup milestone are implemented by one PR.
Runtime lifecycle uses modules.enabled as the single repo-owned module selection authority.
Preset/profile/feature-tier/bootstrapper framing is removed from executable selection, registry contract, primary docs, and generic tests.
Lifecycle and reports distinguish available, enabled, installed/current, missing, and residue module states.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented config-owned core module enablement for the module lifecycle cleanup milestone, removed preset/profile/feature-tier selection from runtime contracts and docs, regenerated package outputs, and updated lifecycle/reporting tests.
execution_run.scope touched: Workspace config/runtime/contracts/generated packages, module registry schemas, public docs, package installer guidance, and focused tests.
execution_run.changed surfaces: src/agentic_workspace, src/agentic_workspace/contracts, generated, docs, README.md, packages/memory, packages/planning, tests, .agentic-workspace planning state.

finished_run_review.scope respected: Scope stayed within the milestone: modules.enabled is now the repo-owned selection authority; presets are rejected; lifecycle state distinguishes enabled, installed/current, missing, residue, and available modules.
